heading: Taxes on failure to distribute income
label: 4942 Taxes on failure to distribute income
notes: CodificationSections 1212(b) and 1244(a) of , which directed the amendment of section 4942 without specifying the act to be amended, were executed to this section, which is section 4942 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, to reflect the probable intent of Congress. See 2006 Amendment notes below.Pub. L. 109–280

children.text: for any taxable year for which it is an operating foundation (as defined in subsection (j)(3)), or

children.label: A
children.text: the failure to value the assets properly was not willful and was due to reasonable cause,

children.label: B
children.text: such amount is distributed as qualifying distributions (within the meaning of subsection (g)) by the foundation during the allowable distribution period (as defined in subsection (j)(2)),

children.label: C
children.text: the foundation notifies the Secretary that such amount has been distributed (within the meaning of subparagraph (B)) to correct such failure, and

children.label: D
children.text: such distribution is treated under subsection (h)(2) as made out of the undistributed income for the taxable year for which a tax would (except for this paragraph) have been imposed under this subsection.

children.heading: Additional tax
children.label: b Additional tax
children.text: In any case in which an initial tax is imposed under subsection (a) on the undistributed income of a private foundation for any taxable year, if any portion of such income remains undistributed at the close of the taxable period, there is hereby imposed a tax equal to 100 percent of the amount remaining undistributed at such time.
the distributable amount for such taxable year, exceeds
the qualifying distributions made before such time out of such distributable amount.
the sum of the minimum investment return plus the amounts described in subsection (f)(2)(C), reduced by
the sum of the taxes imposed on such private foundation for the taxable year under subtitle A and section 4940.

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate fair market value of all assets of the foundation other than those which are used (or held for use) directly in carrying out the foundation’s exempt purpose, over

children.label: B
children.text: the acquisition indebtedness with respect to such assets (determined under section 514(c)(1) without regard to the taxable year in which the indebtedness was incurred).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1)(A), the fair market value of securities for which market quotations are readily available shall be determined on a monthly basis. For all other assets, the fair market value shall be determined at such times and in such manner as the Secretary shall by regulations prescribe.

children.heading: Reductions in value for blockage or similar factors
children.label: B Reductions in value for blockage or similar factors
i
children.text: the size of the block of such securities,

children.label: ii
children.text: the fact that the securities held are securities in a closely held corporation, or

children.label: iii
children.text: the fact that the sale of such securities would result in a forced or distress sale,

children.label: A
children.text: the gross income for the taxable year (determined with the income modifications provided by paragraph (2)), over

children.label: B
children.text: the sum of the deductions (determined with the deduction modifications provided by paragraph (3)) which would be allowed to a corporation subject to the tax imposed by section 11 for the taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: section 103 (relating to State and local bonds) shall not apply,

children.label: B
children.text: capital gains and losses from the sale or other disposition of property shall be taken into account only in an amount equal to any net short-term capital gain for the taxable year;

children.label: i
children.text: amounts received or accrued as repayments of amounts which were taken into account as a qualifying distribution within the meaning of subsection (g)(1)(A) for any taxable year;

children.label: ii
children.text: notwithstanding subparagraph (B), amounts received or accrued from the sale or other disposition of property to the extent that the acquisition of such property was taken into account as a qualifying distribution (within the meaning of subsection (g)(1)(B)) for any taxable year; and

children.label: iii
children.text: any amount set aside under subsection (g)(2) to the extent it is determined that such amount is not necessary for the purposes for which it was set aside; and

children.label: D
children.text: section 483 (relating to imputed interest) shall not apply in the case of a binding contract made in a taxable year beginning before .January 1, 1970

children.label: A
children.text: no deduction shall be allowed other than all the ordinary and necessary expenses paid or incurred for the production or collection of gross income or for the management, conservation, or maintenance of property held for the production of such income and the allowances for depreciation and depletion determined under section 4940(c)(3)(B), and

children.label: B
children.text: section 265 (relating to expenses and interest relating to tax-exempt interest) shall not apply.

children.heading: Transitional rule
children.label: 4 Transitional rule
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (2)(B), the basis (for purposes of determining gain) of property held by a private foundation on , and continuously thereafter to the date of its disposition, shall be deemed to be not less than the fair market value of such property on .December 31, 1969December 31, 1969

children.label: A
children.text: any amount (including that portion of reasonable and necessary administrative expenses) paid to accomplish one or more purposes described in section 170(c)(2)(B), other than any contribution to (i) an organization controlled (directly or indirectly) by the foundation or one or more disqualified persons (as defined in section 4946) with respect to the foundation, except as provided in paragraph (3), or (ii) a private foundation which is not an operating foundation (as defined in subsection (j)(3)), except as provided in paragraph (3), or

children.label: B
children.text: any amount paid to acquire an asset used (or held for use) directly in carrying out one or more purposes described in section 170(c)(2)(B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to such terms and conditions as may be prescribed by the Secretary, an amount set aside for a specific project which comes within one or more purposes described in section 170(c)(2)(B) may be treated as a qualifying distribution if it meets the requirements of subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: at the time of the set-aside the private foundation establishes to the satisfaction of the Secretary that the project is one which can better be accomplished by such set-aside than by immediate payment of funds, or

children.label: I
children.text: the project will not be completed before the end of the taxable year of the foundation in which the set-aside is made,

children.label: II
children.text: the private foundation in each taxable year beginning after (or after the end of the fourth taxable year following the year of its creation, whichever is later), distributes amounts, in cash or its equivalent, equal to not less than the distributable amount determined under subsection (d) (without regard to subsection (i)) for purposes described in section 170(c)(2)(B) (including but not limited to payments with respect to set-asides which were treated as qualifying distributions in one or more prior years), andDecember 31, 1975

children.label: III
children.text: the private foundation has distributed (including but not limited to payments with respect to set-asides which were treated as qualifying distributions in one or more prior years) during the four taxable years immediately preceding its first taxable year beginning after , or the fifth taxable year following the year of its creation, whichever is later, an aggregate amount, in cash or its equivalent, of not less than the sum of the following: 80 percent of the first preceding taxable year’s distributable amount; 60 percent of the second preceding taxable year’s distributable amount; 40 percent of the third preceding taxable year’s distributable amount; and 20 percent of the fourth preceding taxable year’s distributable amount.December 31, 1975

children.label: i
children.text: the failure to distribute such amounts was not willful and was due to reasonable cause, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the foundation distributes an amount in cash or its equivalent which is not less than the difference between the amounts required to be distributed under clause (ii)(II) of subparagraph (B) and the amounts actually distributed in cash or its equivalent during that taxable year within the correction period (as defined in section 4963(e)),

children.heading: Reduction in distribution amount
children.label: D Reduction in distribution amount
children.text: If, during the taxable years in the adjustment period for which the organization is a private foundation, the foundation distributes amounts in cash or its equivalent which exceed the amount required to be distributed under clause (ii)(II) of subparagraph (B) (including but not limited to payments with respect to set-asides which were treated as qualifying distributions in prior years), then for purposes of this subsection the distribution required under clause (ii)(II) of subparagraph (B) for the taxable year shall be reduced by an amount equal to such excess.

children.heading: Adjustment period
children.label: E Adjustment period
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (D), with respect to any taxable year of a private foundation, the taxable years in the adjustment period are the taxable years (not exceeding 5) beginning after , and immediately preceding the taxable year.December 31, 1975

children.label: A
children.text: not later than the close of the first taxable year after its taxable year in which such contribution is received, such organization makes a distribution equal to the amount of such contribution and such distribution is a qualifying distribution (within the meaning of paragraph (1) or (2), without regard to this paragraph) which is treated under subsection (h) as a distribution out of corpus (or would be so treated if such section 501(c)(3) organization were a private foundation which is not an operating foundation), and

children.label: B
children.text: the private foundation making the contribution obtains adequate records or other sufficient evidence from such organization showing that the qualifying distribution described in subparagraph (A) has been made by such organization.

children.heading: Limitation on distributions by nonoperating private foundations to supporting organizations
children.label: 4 Limitation on distributions by nonoperating private foundations to supporting organizations
i
children.text: any type III supporting organization (as defined in section 4943(f)(5)(A)) which is not a functionally integrated type III supporting organization (as defined in section 4943(f)(5)(B)), and

children.label: I
children.text: a disqualified person of the private foundation directly or indirectly controls such organization or a supported organization (as defined in section 509(f)(3)) of such organization, or

children.label: II
children.text: the Secretary determines by regulations that a distribution to such organization otherwise is inappropriate.

children.heading: Type I and type II supporting organizations
children.label: B Type I and type II supporting organizations
i
children.text: operated, supervised, or controlled by one or more organizations described in paragraph (1) or (2) of section 509(a), or

children.label: ii
children.text: supervised or controlled in connection with one or more such organizations.

children.heading: Functionally integrated type III supporting organizations
children.label: C Functionally integrated type III supporting organizations
children.text: An organization is described in this subparagraph if the organization is a functionally integrated type III supporting organization (as defined under section 4943(f)(5)(B)).

children.label: A
children.text: first out of the undistributed income of the immediately preceding taxable year (if the private foundation was subject to the tax imposed by this section for such preceding taxable year) to the extent thereof,

children.label: B
children.text: second out of the undistributed income for the taxable year to the extent thereof, and

children.label: C
children.text: then out of corpus.

children.heading: Correction of deficient distributions for prior taxable years, etc.
children.label: 2 Correction of deficient distributions for prior taxable years, etc.
children.text: In the case of any qualifying distribution which (under paragraph (1)) is not treated as made out of the undistributed income of the immediately preceding taxable year, the foundation may elect to treat any portion of such distribution as made out of the undistributed income of a designated prior taxable year or out of corpus. The election shall be made by the foundation at such time and in such manner as the Secretary shall by regulations prescribe.

children.heading: Adjustment of distributable amount where distributions during prior years have exceeded income
children.label: i Adjustment of distributable amount where distributions during prior years have exceeded income
A
children.text: the aggregate qualifying distributions treated (under subsection (h)) as made out of the undistributed income for such taxable year or as made out of corpus (except to the extent subsection (g)(3) with respect to the recipient private foundation or section 170(b)(1)(F)(ii) applies) during such taxable years, exceed

children.label: B
children.text: the distributable amounts for such taxable years (determined without regard to this subsection),

children.heading: Taxable years in adjustment period
children.label: 2 Taxable years in adjustment period
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), with respect to any taxable year of a private foundation the taxable years in the adjustment period are the taxable years (not exceeding 5) immediately preceding the taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: the date of mailing of a notice of deficiency with respect to the tax imposed by subsection (a) under section 6212, or

children.label: B
children.text: the date on which the tax imposed by subsection (a) is assessed.

children.label: A
children.text: any period in which a deficiency cannot be assessed under section 6213(a), and

children.label: B
children.text: any other period which the Secretary determines is reasonable and necessary to permit a distribution of undistributed income under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: its adjusted net income (as defined in subsection (f)), or

children.label: ii
children.text: its minimum investment return; and

children.label: i
children.text: substantially more than half of the assets of which are devoted directly to such activities or to functionally related businesses (as defined in paragraph (4)), or to both, or are stock of a corporation which is controlled by the foundation and substantially all of the assets of which are so devoted,

children.label: ii
children.text: which normally makes qualifying distributions (within the meaning of paragraph (1) or (2) of subsection (g)) directly for the active conduct of the activities constituting the purpose or function for which it is organized and operated in an amount not less than two-thirds of its minimum investment return (as defined in subsection (e)), or

children.label: iii
children.text: substantially all of the support (other than gross investment income as defined in section 509(e)) of which is normally received from the general public and from 5 or more exempt organizations which are not described in section 4946(a)(1)(H) with respect to each other or the recipient foundation; not more than 25 percent of the support (other than gross investment income) of which is normally received from any one such exempt organization; and not more than half of the support of which is normally received from gross investment income.

children.label: A
children.text: a trade or business which is not an unrelated trade or business (as defined in section 513), or

children.label: B
children.text: an activity which is carried on within a larger aggregate of similar activities or within a larger complex of other endeavors which is related (aside from the need of the organization for income or funds or the use it makes of the profits derived) to the exempt purposes of the organization.

children.heading: Certain elderly care facilities
children.label: 5 Certain elderly care facilities
children.text: For purposes of this section (but no other provisions of this title), the term “operating foundation” includes any organization which, on , and at all times thereafter before the close of the taxable year, operated and maintained as its principal functional purpose facilities for the long-term care, comfort, maintenance, or education of permanently and totally disabled persons, elderly persons, needy widows, or children but only if such organization meets the requirements of paragraph (3)(B)(ii).May 26, 1969
